analysis: Seven nights, and the range on you is genuinely wild — from nearly six hours to over nine, with most nights landing in that decent-but-not-quite zone of seven to seven and a half. The opening night of this stretch was your strongest by far, a proper 8h 27m with almost two hours of REM and nearly an hour of deep sleep, which means the stack of interventions you brought into this window absolutely can deliver. The middle of the week dipped hard — that 5h 57m night had only 28 minutes of deep sleep, which is the lowest deep of the whole run and probably left you feeling it — but you bounced back the next night with 9h 22m and your best REM of the week at 2h 12m, so your body clearly knows how to recover when you let it. The tail end of the week has crept back down toward seven hours with deep sleep dropping again, so the thing worth watching isn't the total so much as protecting the conditions that got you that strong first night.
